CREATE TABLE test_times (id INT PRIMARY KEY, my_time TIME);
INSERT INTO test_times VALUES (1, TIME '12:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_times VALUES (2, TIME '23:59:59');
INSERT INTO test_times VALUES (3, TIME '00:00:01');
SELECT sql_add(my_time, INTERVAL '1' SECOND) FROM test_times WHERE id = 1;
SELECT sql_add(my_time, INTERVAL '60' SECOND) FROM test_times WHERE id = 2;
SELECT sql_add(my_time, INTERVAL '59' SECOND) FROM test_times WHERE id = 3;
CREATE TABLE date_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, my_date DATE);
INSERT INTO date_test VALUES (1, DATE '2023-01-01');
INSERT INTO date_test VALUES (2, DATE '2023-12-31');
SELECT sql_add(my_date, INTERVAL '1' SECOND) FROM date_test WHERE id = 1;
SELECT sql_add(my_date, INTERVAL '60' SECOND) FROM date_test WHERE id = 2;
CREATE TABLE timestamp_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, my_timestamp TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO timestamp_test VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 01:00:00');
INSERT INTO timestamp_test VALUES (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-12-31 23:59:59');
SELECT sql_add(my_timestamp, INTERVAL '1' SECOND) FROM timestamp_test WHERE id = 1;
SELECT sql_add(my_timestamp, INTERVAL '120' SECOND) FROM timestamp_test WHERE id = 2;
CREATE TABLE combined_test AS SELECT t1.id, sql_add(t1.my_time, INTERVAL '15' MINUTE) AS modified_time FROM test_times t1;
CREATE TABLE complex_test AS SELECT dt.id, sql_add(dt.my_date, INTERVAL '30' DAY) AS modified_date FROM date_test dt INNER JOIN combined_test ct ON dt.id = ct.id;
SELECT * FROM combined_test WHERE modified_time > TIME '13:00:00';
SELECT * FROM complex_test WHERE modified_date < DATE '2023-02-01';
